title: hieracium blekingense
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", hieracium blekingense benefits may be related to its protoanemonin content, which could positively influence the healing process, potentially making it useful in certain modern medical treatments.
side_effects: Hieracium blekingense can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Overconsumption of the plant can lead to stomach cramps, diarrhea, and vomiting due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. In rare cases, prolonged exposure to the plant's chemical compounds may cause skin irritation, redness, and inflammation. If ingested in large quantities or without proper guidance, Hieracium blekingense can also cause kidney and liver damage.
